title: Tabernaemontana daktoensis

The genus Tabernaemontana is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The classification of Tabernaemontana daktoensis provides valuable insights into its evolutionary history and relationships with other species.

intro: Tabernaemontana dactyloides, also known as the yellow chalice vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia and Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
